qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in the GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint via direct axios.get() without SSRF validation, which is a different vulnerability class and different component from the advisory-described Python Task Runner sandbox escape. The advisory specifically concerns the Python Code Node sandbox escape on the task runner container, while this finding concerns SSRF in the workflow import-from-url REST endpoint. Different root cause, different vulnerable code path, different attack surface.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Python Task Runner sandbox escape vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-44v6-jhgm-p3m4) describes a sandbox escape in the Python Code Node that allows authenticated users to achieve arbitrary code execution on the task runner container when the Python Task Runner is enabled. The 'ssrf-workflow-import-from-url' finding describes SSRF in the workflow import-from-url endpoint, which is a different vulnerability class affecting a different component with a different root cause.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-44v6-jhgm-p3m4 describes Python Task Runner sandbox escape (CWE-94) in the Python Code Node
Advisory requires: authenticated user + Python Task Runner enabled + workflow creation permission
Advisory impact: arbitrary code execution on task runner container via sandbox escape
Finding 'ssrf-workflow-import-from-url': SSRF in from-url endpoint - different component, different root cause
Finding does not mention Python Code Node, Python Task Runner, or sandbox escape mechanisms
